analyst.why: Ranked by data consistency: supply exhaustion visible in hold pattern, institutional bid inferred from floor, macro relief secondary.

narrative.opinion: Post-capitulation exhaustion: the selling pressure has temporarily dried up after the flush, but there is no fresh bid to drive a sustained recovery. ETF flow stagnation: institutional flows are not providing the tailwind needed to flip the trend, leaving the market vulnerable to drift. Macro uncertainty: lingering rate-cut repricing keeps risk assets on edge, capping upside enthusiasm.
narrative.why: These three forces best explain the transition from sharp selling to weak, unconvincing chop.

judge.why: These are the primary forces driving the current market movement.
